purpose: Define mandatory acquisition-route recording rules before continuing C-rank coverage and later A-rank upgrades.
mandatory_rule: No promoted C-rank or A-rank record set may be added unless its acquisition path is recorded well enough for the next update to re-fetch current real data without restarting the investigation from scratch.

promotion_status_values.ready_for_next_update: Promoted records exist and the source route is documented enough to re-run the next month/update.
promotion_status_values.progress_not_ready_for_calendar: Some real source evidence exists, but promoted records are blocked by incomplete coverage, missing racecourse/date text, visible-window limits, login barriers, or unresolved parser route.
promotion_status_values.route_only_not_ready: A possible official route exists, but no usable month/data rows have been captured yet.

blocked_record_policy: Do not promote inferred dates or inferred racecourses.
Do not promote visible-window rows as full-month coverage.
Do not promote login-only or unverified snippets unless a reusable public or accepted capture route is documented.
Do not use fixture/sample/mock/needs_review/not_checked placeholders as promoted records.
When a source cannot produce C-rank fields, record the checked route and blocker reason in progress files.
next_phase_order: First: complete C-rank coverage or blockers for every target system.
Second: update acquisition readiness so every promoted/blocker route is reusable.
Third: design A-rank upgrades only after C-rank coverage is complete.
Fourth: promote A-rank data system-by-system, only with reusable acquisition routes.
